title: Sid Meier's Civilization® V

notes:
The game works natively but as other reports here show, it crashes numerous times during game play at random intervals. I thought I'd post what resolved the issue for me so others can see, although I found the fix from a Reddit post.

You will need to modify the games .ini configuration file, located - 

/home/username/.local/share/Aspyr/Sid Meier's Civilization 5/config.ini

Modify the parameter "MaxSimultaneousThreads" from the default 8 to 16. This may reflect my CPU available threads.

After saving that change, I no longer crashed. Hope this helps others having this issue using the native client.
title: Sid Meier's Civilization® V

title: Sid Meier's Civilization® V

notes:
Using native version. Just noting what worked for me to get game stable on native.
Had to set a launch option of 
LD_PRELOAD=/usr/lib32/libopenal.so.1 %command%
to get the game to launch. To get the game to stay stable, had to go to /home/(username)/.local/share/Aspyr/Sid Meier's Civilization 5/config.ini 
And change MaxSimultaneousThreads to 16 (you want to set this to your max number of cpu threads. I set it to 16 even though mine is 24 and it tends to work fine though.) 
If your crashing on start, do the first thing, if your crashing randomly after ending your turn, do the 2nd thing.
title: Sid Meier's Civilization® V

notes: Game shows black screen followed by ctd running native and does nothing on all versions of proton.
title: Sid Meier's Civilization® V

notes:
Used a mixture of launch options from here and from a forum post to get the game starting and stable. 
If you have issues getting the game to start try the libopenal launch option. If the game randomly crashes try the taskset launch option, some people have said to try changing the config but changing the config did not work for me,  using tasket did fix the issue for me. All of this was on the native version.
LD_PRELOAD=/usr/lib32/libopenal.so.1 taskset -c 0-3 %command%
title: Sid Meier's Civilization® V
